title: Lastrea hunsteiniana
intro: Lastrea hunsteiniana, also known as Lastrea hunsteiniana or the Hunstein's Lady fern, is a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Asia, to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea hunsteiniana include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lastrea hunsteiniana is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, as well as being used in the form of a tincture or tablet, to take advantage of its health benefits.
